AWS revenue $29.3B in Q1 (+17% YoY, $117B annual run rate) with CEO Jassy targeting multi-hundred-billion-dollar run rate; advertising and AI are durable growth vectors

AAPL.final_explanation: AI 'Mag 7' leaders face valuation gravity as growth decelerates; AAPL's premium multiple compressing but still demands earnings acceleration that has yet to materialize.

AMGN.final_explanation: Amgen trades at a premium valuation (35.8x PE) despite decelerating growth, extreme leverage (8x D/E), and an undifferentiated obesity pipeline — MariTide Phase 2 data confirmed efficacy but disappointed the market, and no near-term catalyst justifies the multiple.

AMZN.final_explanation: AMZN is a quality compounder with excellent fundamentals (margin expansion, AWS growth, AI tailwinds) but the cheap entry from the April tariff selloff is gone; at 36x P/E and near ATH, risk-adjusted reward is modest.

AXP.final_explanation: AXP near ATH at 21.8x P/E after full tariff-recovery; quality compounder but revenue decelerating and valuation leaves limited upside vs downside risk.

BA.final_explanation: BA's Q1 2025 operating inflection is real — first positive operating income since the crisis — but the 50% price recovery from November lows leaves limited risk-adjusted upside while the balance sheet remains deeply impaired and the Air India crash creates fresh safety overhang.

CAT.final_explanation: CAT has re-rated to 18.6x P/E on deteriorating fundamentals — revenue declining 10%, margins compressing 400bp, tariff headwinds ahead — creating unfavorable risk/reward near all-time highs.

CRM.final_explanation: CRM offers improved risk/reward after a year of P/E compression (62x→41x) with stable margins, but 8% revenue growth and no clear re-rating catalyst keep conviction moderate.

CVX.final_explanation: CVX has corrected from stretched valuation territory but declining earnings and weak oil keep risk/reward balanced; Hess arbitration remains the key binary catalyst.

HON.final_explanation: HON: Breakup catalyst progressing but valuation has re-rated from 22x to 25x P/E, leaving limited risk-adjusted upside as execution complexity from simultaneous three-way separation offsets remaining conglomerate discount.

IBM.final_explanation: IBM is a value trap disguised as an AI play — P/E has tripled from 23x to 51x in a year on narrative alone while earnings declined 21% and margins remain below 10%.

KO.final_explanation: Consumer staples quality compounder fully priced at 28x P/E despite decelerating 2% revenue growth — mild bearish lean on stretched valuation with no catalyst.

MCD.final_explanation: MCD's defensive premium is eroding as U.S. same-store sales post their steepest decline since 2020 while the stock still trades at ~26x P/E on flat-to-declining EPS.

NVDA.final_explanation: NVDA remains the dominant AI infrastructure franchise but the stock has rallied 46% from April lows to near all-time highs at $158, pushing TTM P/E back above 50x — the prior thesis's call for further multiple compression has not materialized, and China export restrictions are proving structural rather than temporary.

PG.final_explanation: PG's valuation has compressed to the low end of its multi-year range after a genuine earnings miss and tariff headwinds, creating a mildly attractive entry for a quality compounder — but the fundamental acceleration needed to justify meaningfully higher prices is not yet visible.

SHW.final_explanation: SHW margin expansion thesis intact — gross margins continue structural improvement driving EPS growth despite soft revenue, while P/E has modestly compressed.

TRV.final_explanation: TRV's valuation case has weakened: P/E expanded from 12.3x to 14.4x on Q1 2025 earnings weakness (EPS $1.70 vs $4.80 YoY), eroding the compressed-multiple thesis that drove prior +0.35 scores.
